text:
A few hours ahead of the Paris Olympics opening ceremony, France's high-speed rail lines were hit by a series of fires, disrupting travel nationwide.

French state rail company SNCF said it is a "massive attack aimed at paralyzing the high-speed line network," and its Atlantic, Northern and Eastern high-speed lines were damaged in the arson attacks.

"A large number of trains were diverted or canceled," it wrote on X and urged "all travelers who can to postpone their trip and not go to the station."

Thousands of customers will be affected with disruption expected through the weekend.

Eurostar said a quarter of its services will be cancelled until Monday.

The joint French-Swiss airport Basel-Mulhouse was evacuated and closed on Friday morning for safety reasons.



French transport minister condemned the "co-ordinated malicious acts."

Despite the disruption, the inaugural ceremony of the Olympic Games will go on as scheduled on the Seine River. At the Parade of Nations, thousands of athletes from participating nations will travel on boats through the heart of the city, waving to fans on the river bank.

The number of attendees at the opening ceremony has been cut short due to security reasons.

In the wake of the attacks, French Interior Minister Gerald Darmanin announced that security has been beefed up. 45,000-strong police force, 10,000 soldiers and 20,000 private security personnel have been deployed for the opening ceremony.

The Paris prosecutor's office said it has launched an investigation into the incidents that started overnight.

In Europe, the economic sentiment data from the euro area was in focus. Further, several European countries released updates for third quarter GDP data. The central bank decision from New Zealand was among the main news in Asia.
title: 'Massive Attack' Paralyzes French Train Network Ahead Of Paris Olympics
description: A few hours ahead of the Paris Olympics opening ceremony, France's high-speed rail lines were hit by a series of fires, disrupting travel nationwide. French state rail company SNCF said it is a "massive attack aimed at paralyzing the high-speed line network," and its Atlantic, Northern and Eastern high-speed lines were damaged in the arson attacks.
keywords: Massive Attack, paris olympics, French train network, high-speed rail lines, SNFC
